rationale: In the literature review, the cited paper is singled out as prior transaction-level evidence that reference prices affect both quantity and transacted prices in B2B markets. The citing paper uses this as a key benchmark to frame its contribution, arguing that its own focus is on advertised reference prices and explicit consumer bargaining behavior. This is more than a passing mention because it helps define the gap and positioning of the new study, even without direct reuse of the cited paper's estimation approach.
relation_description: The citing paper treats Bruno, Che, and Dutta (2012) as an important prior empirical result on reference-price effects in transaction outcomes, then extends that stream by studying advertised reference prices in negotiable consumer retail settings. It adds analysis of negotiation initiation and negotiated discounts, alongside demand and revenue implications under bargaining versus fixed pricing.
